extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT won the Stage 4 time trial, beating teammate ROGLIČ by 4 seconds with YATES surprisingly fast through the intermediate check despite fading on the climb finish.
tactical_patterns: YATES Simon fastest through flat intermediate check before losing 5s on climbing finish reveals weakness under sustained uphill power
Jumbo-Visma 1-2-3 sweep DENNIS-ROGLIČ-VAN AERT demonstrates deliberate team depth deployment to control GC through TT specialists
KÜNG Stefan losing 4s to DENNIS despite European TT title suggests form deficit heading into this parcours type
LAPORTE Christophe lost 30s defending yellow jersey indicates climber-weighted TT course exposes pure sprinters even over short distance

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Beat Olympic champion ROGLIČ on climbing TT finish, marking return to top time trial form
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Fastest through flat intermediate check ahead of specialists signals best TT form in years despite fading on climb
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Four seconds behind VAN AERT shows consistent form but not dominant on climbing finish despite Olympic pedigree
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: Held early lead and finished third, 4s up on KÜNG shows sustained peak TT form
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: negative: Lost 30s and yellow jersey on climbing TT despite 1s overnight lead exposes TT weakness
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: neutral: Lost 4s to DENNIS despite European champion status suggests form slightly below peak
